2233-james-tiberius-kirk-is-born-to-geor.note: Wikipedia marks its second 2233 block as alternate-timeline, covering Kirk's birth aboard the Kelvin. This Memory Alpha record is the prime-timeline birth in Riverside, Iowa, cited by TOS, ENT, SNW and Star Trek IV. Overlay match was a false positive.

2258-christopher-pike-returns-to-the-ent.note: Cited only by DIS 'Such Sweet Sorrow', which is prime canon. The overlay's alternate-timeline claim came from an adjacent Wikipedia block and does not apply.

2264-in-the-same-timeline-commander-thel.note: Not an overlay conflict - detectTimeline() misclassified this directly. The bullet reads 'In the same timeline, Commander Thelin becomes the first officer...' immediately after the preceding bullet ('In an alternate timeline, Kirk commands the Enterprise'), a back-reference detectTimeline()'s phrase list doesn't recognize. This is TAS 'Yesteryear' - the timeline where young Spock died and Thelin replaces him as first officer. Confirmed only one such back-reference exists dataset-wide, so a one-off correction here rather than a parser heuristic.

2266-dr-mccoy-spills-acid-in-sickbay-an-.note: Not an overlay conflict - detectTimeline() matched the literal phrase 'mirror universe' anywhere in the text, but here it appears only in an italicized aside ('an event which also apparently happens on the mirror universe ISS Enterprise'). The primary clause - McCoy spilling acid in sickbay - is the prime event; the raw wikitext groups this bullet under the prime ;{{USS|Enterprise|NCC-1701}} ship heading, not any mirror-universe subsection. Citing TOS 'Mirror, Mirror' isn't corroboration either, per the same rule as Kelvin films: the episode that introduces a divergent universe still narrates prime-side events too. Confirmed only one bullet dataset-wide uses this aside phrasing, so a one-off correction rather than a parser heuristic.

2367-in-another-quantum-reality-picard-i.note: Not an overlay conflict - detectTimeline() matched the phrase 'alternate reality' (MA's idiom for the Kelvin timeline) and tagged this kelvin, but this bullet is TNG 'Parallels', a quantum-multiverse story with no Kelvin-film citation at all: 'In another quantum reality, Picard is lost... In the same alternate reality, Worf and Deanna Troi...'. Confirmed via data/events.raw.json: of 31 'alternate reality' occurrences dataset-wide, the rest are either genuine Kelvin-timeline USS Enterprise headers (2255-2263) correctly caught by the phrase rule, or already protected by an '=== Alternate timelines ===' section heading that overrides phrase matching before detectTimeline() ever runs (see sectionTimeline() in parse-year.js) - e.g. the 2382 'everything was purple' Lower Decks bullet. This 2367 bullet is the one exception: an inline italicized aside with no section heading of its own, so a one-off correction rather than a parser change.

2404-by-this-year-the-doctor-had-named-h.note: Not an overlay conflict - detectTimeline() saw no alternate/mirror/kelvin phrase in this bullet's own text and defaulted to prime, but it's a '**' sub-bullet nested directly under a '*' intro line reading 'In an alternate timeline in which the USS Voyager does not enter the Borg transwarp conduit network and return to Earth in 2378:' on the 2404 year page. There's no H2/H3 section heading here for sectionTimeline() to catch (it's bullet-level markup, not a heading), so the classification falls through to per-bullet phrase matching, which finds nothing in this child bullet's own sentence. Confirmed via data/events.raw.json this is the only 'alternate timeline intro + nested child bullets lacking their own phrase' case dataset-wide (grepped all 30 'In an(other) alternate timeline' occurrences for a following '**' line), so a one-off correction rather than a parser change.

2404-vice-admiral-kathryn-janeway-steals.note: Same '**' nested-bullet pattern as the 'Doctor named himself Joe' entry immediately above, under the same alternate-timeline intro line on the 2404 year page. See that entry's note for the full reasoning.
